data_gaps: red-zone target share IS now retrievable (D-017) and drives the changes vs v1.0 -- notably Drake London UP (0.397 rz share, pool-biggest goal-line role) and Zay Flowers DOWN (0.108 rz share, empty-calorie volume)
route_participation still not retrievable (needs nflverse participation/nextgen)
injury status unavailable in frozen world (Godwin/Rice/Diggs/Aiyuk/Olave/Shaheed recoveries unverifiable)
rookies with no NFL usage data (confidence capped): McMillan, Hunter, Golden, Egbuka, J.Higgins, T.Harris, Burden, K.Williams; Kyle Williams also absent from NE chart

rankings.rationale: 27.2% target share on 175 targets, RISING to 30.9% at 29.2 PPG over the last 8, in the pool's most pass-heavy offense (63.2%). The 17 TDs regress; the target monopoly doesn't.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share below 24% in September.

rankings.rationale: 30.1% target share on 154 targets at 93.2% snaps -- the pool's second-best target funnel, held late. The unproven new QB changes the thrower, not the share.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 25% with the new QB through Week 4.

rankings.rationale: The pool's highest target share (35.7%, 170 targets in 15 games) at 90.6% snaps -- a rookie-year funnel that is pure opportunity. QB instability caps efficiency, not volume.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 28% across Weeks 1-3.

rankings.rationale: A 39.1% last-8 target share -- the highest windowed share in the pool -- at 32.5% season share. Kupp's 214 vacated targets consolidate the funnel further; the 11-game season is the availability caveat.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Adams absorbing 28%+ of targets (down 3-5); another soft-tissue absence (down).

rankings.rationale: UP: 32 red-zone targets (pool-most) at a 0.299 red-zone share (ratio 1.12) with 12 TDs -- elite goal-line volume AND conversion in the pool's highest-volume offense. The red-zone data confirms his scoring role is as secure as his overall target volume.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: New-coordinator share below 22%.

rankings.rationale: 28.3% share on 152 targets in a 59.8%-pass offense; Pickens' arrival takes deep targets but Dallas also vacated 135. Elite, stable volume.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 24% with Pickens active.

rankings.rationale: UP from v1.0 #7: the pool's single biggest goal-line role -- a 0.397 red-zone target share (27 red-zone targets, 9 TD) against a 0.282 overall share, a ratio of 1.41 that dwarfs every other WR. His late-2024 Penix window already trended up; the red-zone data shows that target growth is concentrated exactly where touchdowns come from.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 24% in the full-season Penix offense.

rankings.rationale: 24.5% share at 17.6 PPG when active, and Diggs' exit vacated 105 targets -- the WR1 funnel is his; 12-game 2024 availability is the discount.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 22% as rookies integrate.

rankings.rationale: DOWN slightly: still elite volume (0.339 target share), but a red-zone target share of only 0.20 (ratio 0.59, 11 red-zone targets) -- his massive overall volume was NOT goal-line-concentrated, and the 7 TDs came efficiently on few red-zone looks (regresses down). In the league's most run-heavy offense, the capped goal-line role is a real ceiling note.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: PHI pass rate above 47% (up 3); share below 27% (down).

rankings.rationale: A 32.0% last-8 target share at 19.3 PPG (season 25.5%) -- the pool's steepest ascending rookie trend. The new staff and Hunter's arrival are the unknowns.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Hunter cutting his share below 24% early.

rankings.rationale: UP: a 0.325 red-zone target share (27 red-zone targets, ratio 1.10) with 8 TDs -- a genuine goal-line magnet whose scoring role transfers to the Rams offense that vacated 214 targets. The red-zone data reinforces the volume case.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 22% share in his first month as a Ram.

rankings.rationale: 26.1% share at 18.5 PPG across 12 games with 10 TDs I regress -- elite per-game volume shaded by a 12-and-14-game availability run and Chase's 30%+ share ceiling on the other side.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 17-game pace September at 24%+ share moves him top-8.

rankings.rationale: 23.8% share rising to 25.6% late at 86.4% snaps, with Metcalf and Lockett vacating 199 targets -- though Kupp's arrival claims part of them. Net trend up.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Kupp out-targeting him in September.

rankings.rationale: DOWN slightly: a 0.143 red-zone target share (ratio 0.58) -- his rookie production was between-the-20s volume, and the 7 TDs on only 10 red-zone targets is high efficiency that regresses down. The alpha-role thesis holds, but the TD-upside piece was thinner than the raw share suggested.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 20% as Harris/rookies integrate.

rankings.rationale: 23.3% share on 117 targets with 13 TDs -- the TDs regress hard, the share holds, and Washington vacated 118 targets while adding Deebo.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Deebo eating into a sub-20% share.

rankings.rationale: 25.0% share at 19.3 last-8 PPG with 11 TDs regressing; Godwin's recovery timeline and Egbuka's arrival redistribute, but the WR1 role held every prior year.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 21% with Godwin healthy.

rankings.rationale: 25.9% share on 153 targets at a 96.3% snap share -- volume locked -- though the late window sagged (22.0%, 11.9 PPG) amid QB churn. New QB, same funnel.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 22% with the new QB.

rankings.rationale: A 28.2% share at 19.7 PPG across 7 games before the season-ending injury -- elite slot volume IF health returns, which my data cannot verify; Egbuka's arrival hedges the team's bet and mine.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Missing Week 1 or a snap share under 70% (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: UP from v1.0 #23: a 0.291 red-zone target share (ratio 1.12, 23 red-zone targets) with 8 TDs on top of the pool's highest air-yards share -- a genuine goal-line AND deep-target role, exactly the TD-secure profile the red-zone data was built to surface. v1.0 underrated him on overall volume alone.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 21% as the rookie class integrates.

rankings.rationale: 22.7% share on 145 targets surging to 27.6% at 19.8 PPG over the last 8 (post-Cooper) -- Cleveland's 258 vacated targets keep that funnel his regardless of who plays QB.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 22% in September.

rankings.rationale: 27.1% share on 140 targets at 93.8% snaps, rising late (30.1%) -- but Chicago added Odunze's growth, Burden, and Loveland to the same pie under a new staff.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 22% in the new offense.

rankings.rationale: 24.6% share at 16.2 PPG in his 4 games before injury -- per-game volume that led KC's receivers -- and 163 vacated targets await. A 4-game sample plus unverifiable recovery caps confidence.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A healthy Week 1-2 at 22%+ share (up 5+); limited snaps (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: 28.0% target share when active -- but the same 41.9%-pass, smallest-pie problem as Brown, from the WR2 chair, across a 13-game season.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: PHI pass rate above 47% (up 3).

rankings.rationale: 25.2% share on 129 targets at 91.7% snaps -- quietly a top-12 target funnel; the new regime and a run-tilting rookie back are the unknowns.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 20% under the new staff.

rankings.rationale: 21.7% share on 122 targets -- still real volume despite the down year, with a mild late rebound (14.8 PPG). The trend is down but the floor share held every week.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 18% -- the age-cliff signal my data would show.

rankings.rationale: 20.8% share now moved to Pittsburgh, which vacated 224 targets (Pickens) in a 48.4%-pass offense -- a bigger share of a smaller pie; the trade signals feature intent.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 22% share in the new offense by October.

rankings.rationale: 21.2% share rising to 25.0% at 17.7 PPG over the last 8, with 9 TDs regressing -- solid WR2 volume behind Jefferson's monopoly.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 17% with the new QB.

rankings.rationale: DOWN slightly: a 0.116 red-zone target share (ratio 0.62) -- his 1,001 yards and 7 TDs were deep-role, efficiency-driven production on few goal-line looks, which my methodology regresses. The volume is real but the scoring role isn't goal-line-anchored.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: New-coordinator share below 16%.

rankings.rationale: 25.2% share rising to 27.9% late, and Deebo's exit vacated 127 targets -- but Aiyuk's return and Pearsall's growth compete for the same recovered pie.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Aiyuk healthy AND Jennings under 18% share.

rankings.rationale: 17.9% share rising to 19.3% at 13.9 PPG over the last 8, with 163 vacated targets and Rice's recovery unverifiable -- the volume path is open.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Rice healthy and Worthy under 16% share.

rankings.rationale: 22.6% share on 116 targets with a 41.9% air-yards share -- rookie volume was fine; the output (11.6 PPG) wasn't. Role-average efficiency on held volume projects the bounce.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 19% in September.

rankings.rationale: 26.7% share at Pittsburgh, now the Dallas WR2 where 135 targets vacated -- real volume pedigree, but second fiddle to Lamb's 28% for the first time.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A sub-18% share behind Lamb by October.

rankings.rationale: 23.8% share on 120 targets with a 43.2% air-yards share in a slow offense -- the volume is real, the environment and catch rate cap it; a rookie QB cuts both ways.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 19% with the new QB room.

rankings.rationale: DOWN from v1.0 #29: a 0.108 red-zone target share against a 0.254 overall share -- a ratio of 0.43, the pool's worst divergence. His heavy volume is almost entirely between-the-20s (only 8 red-zone targets) in Baltimore's low-volume passing offense, so the TD upside the raw target share implied largely isn't there.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share below 21%, or BAL pass rate under 44%.

rankings.rationale: 26.1% share when active from the slot at only 64.4% snaps -- per-route volume is elite; the QB carousel and two-down role cap the ceiling.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Snap share above 75% (up 5); a run-lean rookie-QB offense (down).

rankings.rationale: 25.2% share on 111 targets at 88.1% snaps with weak output (10.4 PPG) -- volume solid, efficiency and QB play poor; role-average efficiency projects mild recovery.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Downs/rookies pushing his share under 20%.

rankings.rationale: 24.9% share across 12 games at LAR, now in Seattle where 199 targets vacated -- the slot volume transfers in principle; age-decline and the JSN overlap are the risks my data can only hint at (12.2 late PPG).
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 20% share in his first Seattle month.

rankings.rationale: 24.4% share at 15.2 PPG through 8 games before the season-ending injury, now on his second move in two years (NE, chart-confirmed) -- share pedigree against recovery and a low-volume offense.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Full Week 1 snaps (up 3-5); a rehab-limited camp (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: 23.0% share (25.5% late) on 61.3% snaps -- Buffalo's most-targeted receiver per snap, with 99 vacated targets; the slot-only role caps total volume.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Coleman/newcomers pushing his share under 19%.

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data -- confidence capped. Listed WR1 immediately in an offense that vacated 135 targets; the clearest rookie target runway in the pool.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 18% target share by October (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: 20.6% share on 106 targets at 92.8% snaps -- stable WR2 volume behind London, with the late window fading (10.4 PPG) as London's share surged.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: London's growth pushing him under 16%.

rankings.rationale: 19.7% share across an 8-game, twice-concussed season -- his prior seasons ran higher, and New Orleans returns a thin target room; health, not opportunity, is the question my data can't answer.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A healthy September at 24%+ share (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 16.3% share -- a sharp down year with Hill still absorbing 21.7% -- at 78.7% snaps; the volume path requires a Hill decline or exit my data doesn't show.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share above 21% early signals the rebound (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 18.8% share in a down 15-game year, traded to Washington where 118 targets vacated -- a hybrid role reboot with declining receiving usage (14.5% air-yards share).
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 15% share behind McLaurin by October.

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data, and a two-way deployment plan that makes his offensive snap share genuinely unknowable -- the widest outcome range in the pool; Jacksonville's 218 vacated targets are the upside path.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A full-time offensive snap share Week 1 (up 8+); true two-way rotation (down).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data -- capped. Behind Evans and a recovering Godwin on a team that vacated nothing; the path to targets requires an injury ahead of him.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Godwin setback promoting him to full slot snaps (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: 24.4% share with a 50.7% air-yards share in his 6 games -- the deepest role in the pool per target -- before injury; a thin Saints room keeps the role his if healthy.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A healthy 20%+ share September (up 5).

rankings.rationale: 18.9% share on 101 rookie targets at 83.6% snaps with poor efficiency -- volume was fine; the new staff, Burden, and Loveland now crowd the same pie.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share above 23% in the new offense (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 16.9% share with 9 TDs on 72 targets -- TD-dependent scoring that regresses hard in a 46.3%-pass offense; the share itself was flat all year.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share above 20% post-Andrews-decline (up 5).

rankings.rationale: 15.5% rookie share rising to 17.3% late with 99 vacated targets in the room -- modest growth path in a spread-it-around offense.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 14% share by October (down).

rankings.rationale: 16.2% share fading to 14.0% late (8.0 PPG) as the rotation deepened -- and Green Bay drafted Golden into the same room; the trend and the draft signal both point down.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 20%+ share month restoring the 2023 role (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 19.1% share in 8 games before injury, now in Houston where Diggs' exit vacated 105 targets -- a clean slot-volume path behind Collins if healthy.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Under 15% share behind the rookie class (down).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data -- capped. Green Bay vacated zero targets and spreads its passing tree wider than any contender; the first-round capital argues intent, the structure argues patience.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 20%+ target share month (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: 22.2% share in 7 games with zero TDs before the multi-ligament injury -- recovery timeline unverifiable in my data, and Jennings' late surge claims his X role in the interim.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Active Week 1 at 80%+ snaps (up 8+); a PUP start (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data -- capped. Second-round capital into the exact WR2 vacancy Diggs left (105 targets) behind Collins; the cleanest rookie WR2 path in the pool.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Kirk winning the slot AND Higgins under 12% share (down).

rankings.rationale: 12.8% rookie share on 67.0% snaps -- but Deebo's 127 vacated targets and Aiyuk's unverifiable recovery open real volume; the late-2024 usage never confirmed it.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: An 18%+ share September (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: A 14.4-PPG last 8 on just a 14.3% share and 31.8% snaps -- TD-burst efficiency my rules regress hardest; until the snap share doubles, the role isn't real.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 60%+ snap share Week 1 (up 8+); the burst was the peak (hold).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data -- capped. Third receiver at best in a room with Moore, Odunze, and a rookie TE, under a new staff; 154 vacated targets spread across all of them.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Slot-role usage above 15% share early (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data, listed WR2 behind McConkey in a vacated-volume offense (170 targets) -- a real path, second in line.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Out-targeting McConkey in any September week (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie, no NFL usage data, AND absent from my New England chart -- the pool's lowest-information player; Diggs' arrival further crowds the target tree.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Preseason usage ahead of Boutte/Douglas (up 5+).
